Thymelaea, also known as the paperbush or silver-pod, is a genus of flowering plants in the family Thymelaeaceae. Thymelaea species, such as Thymelaea hirsuta and Thymelaea nitida, are known to have various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may aid in the treatment of skin conditions and wounds.

The bioactive constituents of Thymelaea species include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are thought to contribute to their medicinal properties.
